steps.title: Identify the key observation
steps.content: The key clue is the difference between captive and wild primate studies: captive primates show significantly more right-handedness than wild primates. The passage introduces the blank with “therefore,” indicating this difference is the basis for a logical deduction.

steps.title: Determine what this difference logically suggests
steps.content:
Since captive primates are housed with humans—who are 85-90% right-handed—while wild primates are not, the logical possibility is that human exposure causes captive primates to acquire more right-handed behaviors.

The other choices invoke sample size or methodology rather than explaining why the captive environment would produce more right-handedness.

That confirms D is correct.
